## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-28 00:19:22
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## Task Understanding

Implement 5 requirements for the griffe Python library:

1. **SearchPath struct + finder refactor** (finder.py): Introduce a `SearchPath` NamedTuple, change `_handle_pth_file` and `_handle_editable_module` to return `list[SearchPath]` instead of `list[Path]`, and add meson-python editable loader pattern support.

2. **Yield/YieldFrom extraction** (_values.py): Fix `_extract_yield` to return `"yield"` / `"yield a"` instead of `"None"` / `"a"`, add `_extract_yield_from` for `ast.YieldFrom`, register in `_node_map`.

3. **`inits` parameter for `temporary_pypackage`** (tests.py): Add `inits: bool = True` keyword-only parameter controlling `__init__.py` creation in intermediate directories.

4. **NumPy admonition sections** (numpy.py): Add "see also", "notes", "note", "warnings" to `_section_kind` mapping to `DocstringSectionKind.admonition`, implement `_read_admonition_section`, register in `_section_reader`, pass original-case title to reader.

5. **Dataclass parameters filter** (dataclasses.py): In `Class.parameters`, filter out attributes with "property" label when building dataclass Parameters.

## Proposed Approach

### Req 1 - SearchPath + meson-python
- Define `SearchPath = NamedTuple("SearchPath", [("path", Path), ("always_scan_for", str | None)])` with default `None` at module level in finder.py.
- Wrap all `Path` returns in `_handle_pth_file` with `SearchPath(path=..., always_scan_for=None)`.
- Wrap all `Path` returns in `_handle_editable_module` with `SearchPath(...)`.
- Add meson-python pattern `^_\w+_editable_loader\.py$` and handler: parse file, find `install()` call, extract set of package names and base path, return `SearchPath(path=base/src, always_scan_for=pkg)` for each existing directory.

### Req 2 - Yield extraction
- Fix `_extract_yield`: return `"yield"` when `node.value is None`, return `"yield " + _extract(node.value)` otherwise.
- Add `_extract_yield_from(node)` returning `"yield from " + _extract(node.value)`.
- Register `ast.YieldFrom: _extract_yield_from` in `_node_map`.

### Req 3 - inits parameter
- Add `inits: bool = True` after `init` in signature.
- In the loop creating intermediate dirs, conditionally skip `__init__.py` touch when `inits=False`.

### Req 4 - NumPy admonitions
- Add entries to `_section_kind`: `"see also"`, `"notes"`, `"note"`, `"warnings"` -> `DocstringSectionKind.admonition`.
- Implement `_read_admonition_section(docstring, *, offset, title, **options)` that reads body lines until next section or EOF, returns `DocstringSectionAdmonition(kind=..., text=..., title=title)`.
- Kind mapping: "warnings" -> "warning", "notes"/"note" -> "note", "see also" -> "seealso".
- Register in `_section_reader`.
- Modify parse loop to pass original-case title to reader when section kind is admonition.

### Req 5 - Dataclass property filter
- Add `if "property" not in attr.labels` filter in the list comprehension inside `Class.parameters`.

## Expected Deliverables

- Modified files: `finder.py`, `_values.py`, `tests.py`, `numpy.py`, `dataclasses.py`
- All 20 acceptance tests passing

## Test Plan

- **Test command**: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- **Coverage scope**: All acceptance tests listed in requirements (20 tests across 5 files)
- **Regression**: Full test suite must pass with no new failures

## Quality Gate Commands

- `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q` (tests)

requirements.location:
src/griffe/finder.py — module-level (new named structure) and functions
      _handle_pth_file (line 376), _handle_editable_module (line 399)
requirements.description:
Both _handle_pth_file and _handle_editable_module currently return list[Path].
      However, all test callers access the results via sp.path (e.g.,
      [sp.path for sp in _handle_pth_file(pth_file)]), and one test
      (test_meson_python_file_handling) also accesses sp.always_scan_for.
      Returning plain Path objects causes AttributeError: 'PosixPath' object has
      no attribute 'path' in every affected test.

      Additionally, _handle_editable_module does not recognize the meson-python
      editable file pattern (^_\w+_editable_loader\.py$), causing
      UnhandledEditableModuleError for test_meson_python_file_handling.
requirements.contract:
1. Introduce a new named data structure (e.g., NamedTuple or dataclass)
         called SearchPath in finder.py with at least:
           - path: Path            — the resolved search path
           - always_scan_for: str | None = None
                                  — when set, the finder should only search for
                                    this particular package name in this path

      2. Change the return type of _handle_pth_file from list[Path] to
         list[SearchPath]. Each returned object wraps a directory Path with
         always_scan_for=None.

      3. Change the return type of _handle_editable_module from list[Path] to
         list[SearchPath]. For all existing patterns (editables, setuptools,
         scikit-build-core), wrap each resolved Path as SearchPath(path=...,
         always_scan_for=None).

      4. Add support for the meson-python editable loader pattern inside
         _handle_editable_module:
           - File name pattern: ^_\w+_editable_loader\.py$ (e.g.,
             _whatever_editable_loader.py)
           - File format: contains an install() call whose first argument is a
             set/frozenset literal of package name strings, and whose second
             argument is a base directory path string.
           - Resolved search path: base_path / "src"
           - Return one SearchPath per package name in the set for which the
             directory (base_path / "src" / package_name) exists on the
             filesystem, each with always_scan_for set to that package name
             and path set to (base_path / "src").
           - If no package directories exist, return an empty list (or raise
             UnhandledEditableModuleError).

      Interface signatures (informational, not prescriptive):
        SearchPath: NamedTuple or dataclass with fields path: Path and
                    always_scan_for: str | None = None
        _handle_pth_file(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]
        _handle_editable_module(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]

requirements.location:
src/griffe/agents/nodes/_values.py — _extract_yield (line 292),
      _node_map (line 298), and a new _extract_yield_from function to be added
requirements.description:
The current _extract_yield function returns repr(None) (the string "None")
      when the yield expression has no value, and returns only the yielded
      sub-expression (e.g., "a") when there is one, rather than the full
      "yield" or "yield a" representation.

      Furthermore, ast.YieldFrom is absent from _node_map entirely, so any
      call to get_value() or safe_get_value() on a yield-from node raises
      KeyError: <class 'ast.YieldFrom'>, causing failures in both
      test_building_value_from_nodes and test_building_expressions_from_nodes.
requirements.contract:
1. Fix _extract_yield so that:
           - When node.value is None, return the string "yield" (not "None").
           - When node.value is not None, return "yield " + _extract(node.value),
             e.g., "yield a".

      2. Add a new function _extract_yield_from(node: ast.YieldFrom) -> str
         that returns "yield from " + _extract(node.value), e.g., "yield from a".

      3. Register ast.YieldFrom: _extract_yield_from in _node_map so that
         get_value() and safe_get_value() handle yield-from nodes without error.

      Expected behaviors:
        get_value(compile("yield", mode="exec", ...).body[0].value)   == "yield"
        get_value(compile("yield a", mode="exec", ...).body[0].value) == "yield a"
        get_value(compile("yield from a", mode="exec", ...).body[0].value)
                                                                       == "yield from a"

requirements.description:
temporary_pypackage currently accepts only an init: bool = True parameter
      to control whether the leaf package directory gets an __init__.py file.
      Tests call it with an additional keyword argument inits=..., which is not
      in the current signature, causing:
        TypeError: temporary_pypackage() got an unexpected keyword argument 'inits'
requirements.contract:
Add an inits: bool = True keyword-only parameter to temporary_pypackage.
      This parameter controls whether __init__.py files are created inside
      intermediate subdirectories listed in the modules argument (i.e., the
      directories that are created for non-.py path components).

      When inits=False, intermediate directories should be created (mkdir) but
      should NOT receive an __init__.py, making them namespace packages.
      When inits=True (default), the existing behaviour is preserved: each
      intermediate directory gets an __init__.py.

      The init parameter remains unchanged: it controls __init__.py creation
      only for the top-level leaf package directory (package_path).

      Signature (informational):
        temporary_pypackage(
            package: str,
            modules: Sequence[str] | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
            *,
            init: bool = True,
            inits: bool = True,
        ) -> Iterator[TmpPackage]

requirements.description:
The NumPy docstring parser does not recognise section headers such as
      "See Also", "Notes", "Note", and "Warnings" (case-insensitive) as
      structured admonition sections. These headers are currently treated as
      regular text and appended to the preceding text section.

      As a result, tests expecting two sections (a text section followed by an
      admonition section) observe only one DocstringSectionText section,
      failing len(sections) == 2 assertions. The DocstringSectionAdmonition
      and DocstringAdmonition classes already exist in
      src/griffe/docstrings/dataclasses.py and DocstringSectionKind.admonition
      exists in src/griffe/enumerations.py.
requirements.contract:
1. Add the following lowercase section names to _section_kind, mapping
         each to DocstringSectionKind.admonition:
           "see also"  -> DocstringSectionKind.admonition
           "notes"     -> DocstringSectionKind.admonition
           "note"      -> DocstringSectionKind.admonition
           "warnings"  -> DocstringSectionKind.admonition
           (Other admonition-like names may also be added, but these four are
           the minimum required.)

      2. Implement a _read_admonition_section function that:
           - Reads the block of text following the section header (until the
             next section header or end of docstring).
           - Returns a DocstringSectionAdmonition instance with:
               title set to the original-case section title
                     (e.g., "See Also", "Notes", "Warnings", "Note")
               kind set according to a mapping:
                     "warnings" -> "warning"
                     "notes" / "note" -> "note"
                     "see also" -> "seealso" (or any consistent value;
                                   tests for "See Also" do not assert kind)
               description (value.description) set to the trimmed text
                     content of the section body (empty string if the
                     section body is empty).

      3. Register the new reader in _section_reader under
         DocstringSectionKind.admonition:
           DocstringSectionKind.admonition: _read_admonition_section

      4. The parse loop must supply the original-case section title to the
         reader so the DocstringSectionAdmonition can set title correctly.
         The existing loop uses lines[offset] before lower-casing for section
         detection; this original line should be passed to the reader.

      Behavioural contract:
        - An empty admonition body (nothing after the dash-underline) must
          produce a DocstringSectionAdmonition with value.description == "".
        - "---" lines that appear inside a body (not as a section header) must
          be included as literal text in value.description.
        - Isolated "---" lines (a dash-only line not preceded by a known
          section header) must NOT create a section; they should remain as
          plain text in the surrounding DocstringSectionText.

requirements.description:
The Class.parameters property, when the class has the "dataclass" label,
      builds Parameters from all items in self.attributes.values(). However,
      methods decorated with @property or @cached_property are stored by the
      visitor as Attribute objects with the "property" label (see visitor.py
      line 352-366). These property-decorated members end up in
      self.attributes and are incorrectly included as constructor parameters
      of the dataclass.

      The test asserts that @property and @cached_property members named "a"
      and "b" must NOT appear in the dataclass Parameters.
requirements.contract:
In the Class.parameters property, when constructing Parameters for a
      dataclass (i.e., when "dataclass" in self.labels and no __init__ is
      found in all_members), filter out any Attribute that has "property" in
      its labels before building the Parameter list.

      Only Attribute members without the "property" label (i.e., genuine
      dataclass fields declared as class-level type annotations) should be
      included as parameters.

      Behavioural contract:
        - For a @dataclass class with fields x: float and y: float,
          plus @property def a and @cached_property def b, the resulting
          Parameters must contain x and y but must NOT contain a or b.
        - The fix must not affect dataclasses that have no property members.
        - The fix must not affect classes that have an explicit __init__ method
          (those return __init__.parameters directly).

requirements.location: src/griffe/finder.py — module level (new class) and functions _handle_pth_file, _handle_editable_module
requirements.description:
The functions _handle_pth_file and _handle_editable_module currently return list[Path] (plain
      pathlib.Path objects). However, all tests access elements via .path attribute (e.g.,
      [sp.path for sp in _handle_pth_file(...)]), and the meson-python test additionally accesses
      .always_scan_for (e.g., sp.always_scan_for == "griffe"). Since Path has no .path or
      .always_scan_for attribute, every test that calls these functions fails with
      AttributeError: 'PosixPath' object has no attribute 'path'.

      Additionally, _handle_editable_module does not recognize the meson-python editable loader
      filename pattern (_*_editable_loader.py), so it falls through to raise
      UnhandledEditableModuleError instead of returning search paths.
requirements.contract:
1. Introduce a new class SearchPath (in src/griffe/finder.py, exportable in __all__) with
         at least the following interface:
           - __init__(self, path: Path, always_scan_for: str | None = None) -> None
           - self.path: Path  — the resolved search path
           - self.always_scan_for: str | None  — optional package name hint (None by default)

      2. Update _handle_pth_file(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]:
         Each directory that was previously returned as a bare Path must now be returned as
         SearchPath(path=that_directory). The filtering logic (skip comments, import lines,
         non-existent paths) remains unchanged.

      3. Update _handle_editable_module(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]:
         - Existing "editables" pattern handling: return SearchPath objects wrapping the parent
           directory (previously returned as bare Path).
         - Existing "setuptools" pattern handling: return SearchPath objects wrapping each
           Path(cst.value).parent (previously returned as bare Path).
         - Existing "scikit-build-core" pattern handling: return SearchPath objects wrapping the
           first-dict paths (previously returned as bare Path).
         - NEW "meson-python" pattern: match filenames matching _*_editable_loader.py. Parse the
           file to extract the set of package names (first argument) and the build directory
           (second argument, a string). The actual source root is the build directory joined with
           "src". For each package name in the set, yield a SearchPath whose .path is
           build_directory / "src" and whose .always_scan_for is that package name.
           Only yield paths whose .path exists on disk.

      4. The public __all__ of finder.py must include "SearchPath" so that tests can discover it.

      Return type signature:
        def _handle_pth_file(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]: ...
        def _handle_editable_module(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]: ...

requirements.location:
src/griffe/agents/nodes/_values.py — function _extract_yield (existing, line ~292),
      new function _extract_yieldfrom, and the _node_map dict (line ~298)
requirements.description:
Two related bugs exist in the AST value extraction module:

      1. _extract_yield currently returns repr(None) (the string "None") for a bare "yield"
         expression and returns only the operand string (e.g., "a") for "yield a". Both are
         wrong: the function must include the "yield" keyword in the result.

      2. ast.YieldFrom is completely absent from _node_map, so any attempt to extract a
         "yield from expr" node raises KeyError: <class 'ast.YieldFrom'>.
requirements.contract:
1. Fix _extract_yield(node: ast.Yield, **kwargs) -> str:
         - When node.value is None, return the string "yield" (not "None").
         - When node.value is not None, return "yield " + _extract(node.value, **kwargs).

      2. Add _extract_yieldfrom(node: ast.YieldFrom, **kwargs) -> str:
         - Always return "yield from " + _extract(node.value, **kwargs).

      3. Register ast.YieldFrom in _node_map:
         _node_map[ast.YieldFrom] = _extract_yieldfrom

requirements.location:
src/griffe/docstrings/numpy.py — module-level mapping _section_kind (line ~68),
      a new _read_admonition_section function, _section_reader dict (line ~723),
      and the main parse() function (line ~740)
requirements.description:
The numpy docstring parser's _section_kind dict and _section_reader dict only cover
      structured sections (parameters, returns, raises, etc.). Admonition-style sections
      such as "See Also", "Notes", "Note", "Warnings", and similar headings followed by a
      dash-underline are not recognised. They fall into the else branch and are silently
      merged into the preceding text section, so the resulting sections list has count 1
      instead of the expected 2, causing multiple assertion failures.

      A secondary issue: the main parse() loop checks
        line_lower in _section_kind and _is_dash_line(lines[offset + 1])
      to detect a section start. Isolated dash-only lines (e.g. "---") satisfy
      _is_dash_line but are NOT section headings; they must pass through as plain text
      rather than triggering section parsing.
requirements.contract:
1. Define a mapping _admonition_kind: dict[str, str | None] that maps lowercase section
         names to their admonition kind string (or None if no specific kind applies). At minimum:
           "see also"  -> None  (or "seealso")
           "notes"     -> "note"
           "note"      -> "note"
           "warnings"  -> "warning"
           "warning"   -> "warning"
         Additional common numpy admonition headings (e.g., "references", "todo") may be
         included as appropriate.

      2. Add a function _read_admonition_section(docstring, *, offset, **options) ->
         tuple[DocstringSectionAdmonition, int]:
         - Reads the block of text following the section heading using _read_block().
         - Returns a DocstringSectionAdmonition whose .value.description equals the block
           text (stripped), and whose .value.kind equals the mapped kind (may be None).

      3. Add entries for each key in _admonition_kind to _section_reader, mapped to
         _read_admonition_section, using a synthetic DocstringSectionKind or by extending
         the admonition detection to run in a separate branch before the existing reader lookup.

      4. In the parse() main loop, update the section-start detection so that:
         a. A line qualifies as a section heading only if it is a non-empty, non-dash-only
            string (i.e., _is_dash_line(line) itself is False) AND the next line is a dash
            line. This prevents bare "---" lines from being mistaken for section headings.
         b. If a heading matches _section_kind, the existing structured section reader runs.
         c. If a heading matches _admonition_kind, the admonition reader runs, appending a
            DocstringSectionAdmonition to the sections list with the correct title and kind.
         d. If a heading matches neither, it is treated as plain text (current behaviour for
            unknown headings).

      Key behavioural contracts verified by tests:
        - An empty admonition body (nothing after the dash line) produces a
          DocstringSectionAdmonition with .value.description == "".
        - A "See Also" admonition preserves multi-paragraph body text.
        - "Warnings" maps to kind == "warning"; "Notes" maps to kind == "note".
        - Isolated dash lines inside body text (not preceded by a valid heading) are kept
          as plain text and do NOT split the body into a new section.

requirements.description:
Several tests call temporary_pypackage() with an inits=... keyword argument (e.g.,
      temporary_pypackage("pkg", [...], init=init, inits=init)). The current signature only
      accepts init: bool. When inits is passed, Python raises:
        TypeError: temporary_pypackage() got an unexpected keyword argument 'inits'
      blocking three tests entirely.

      The inits parameter is intended to control whether __init__.py files are created in
      intermediate (sub-)package directories, as opposed to init which controls the leaf
      package. This is needed to create namespace package layouts where intermediate
      directories lack __init__.py.
requirements.contract:
Add an inits: bool = True keyword-only parameter to temporary_pypackage():

        def temporary_pypackage(
            package: str,
            modules: Sequence[str] | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
            *,
            init: bool = True,
            inits: bool = True,
        ) -> Iterator[TmpPackage]: ...

      Semantics of inits:
        - When inits=True (default), the existing behaviour is preserved: __init__.py is
          created in intermediate subdirectories as modules are created.
        - When inits=False, intermediate package directories created while iterating the
          modules list must NOT have __init__.py files added, making them namespace packages.

      The init parameter continues to control __init__.py creation for the top-level leaf
      package directory (the package argument). The inits parameter applies to any
      subdirectories created while processing entries in the modules list.

      The change must be backward-compatible: existing callers that omit inits must see no
      change in behaviour.
